title: No bounds validation on cpuId → CPU_SET undefined behavior
description: case 'a': CPU_SET(cpuId, &cpuSet) with cpuId from std::stoi is undefined behavior for negative values or cpuId >= CPU_SETSIZE (1024). e.g. '-a -1' or '-a 99999'. Fix: validate cpuId against sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN) (and reject negatives) before CPU_SET.

title: mlockall() runs before the daemon fork — locks not inherited across fork()
description: mlockall is at cannelloni.cpp:202, but daemonize() forks later at cannelloni.cpp:440. Per man mlockall, memory locks are NOT inherited across fork(2). So in -f daemon mode the locking is silently ineffective in the child that does the actual work. Fix: perform RT/memory-locking setup AFTER daemonization.

title: mlockall() is unconditional and its return value ignored
description: cannelloni.cpp:202 calls mlockall(MCL_CURRENT|MCL_FUTURE) on EVERY invocation, even without -a/-y, silently changing default behavior for all users and locking all pages into RAM. It typically fails without CAP_IPC_LOCK / RLIMIT_MEMLOCK and the failure is swallowed (return value ignored). Fix: gate behind the RT options (-a/-y) and log the result on failure.

title: PR #84: CPU affinity & real-time priority support
description: Audit of mguentner/cannelloni PR #84 (author: maxwelllls). Adds -a cpu_id, -y priority, unconditional mlockall, and SCHED_FIFO. Builds clean under -Wall -Wextra -Werror and merges clean onto master. RT/affinity settings do propagate to worker threads via inheritance (applied to main thread in getopt; threads created afterward at cannelloni.cpp:494). Verdict: useful feature, NOT mergeable as-is; children 1-4 are correctness/robustness bugs.

title: Debian package (.deb) for cannellonis via CPack
description:
DONE — implemented in CMakeLists.txt via CPack DEB generator.

Result: build/cannellonis_<version>_<arch>.deb
  Package: cannellonis  |  Breaks: cannelloni  |  Replaces: cannelloni
  Section: net  Priority: optional  Homepage: github.com/aalarchiv/cannelloni
  Contents: /usr/bin/cannellonis + /usr/share/doc/cannellonis/{README.md,gpl-2.0.txt}
  Depends: auto via SHLIBDEPS (libc6/libgcc-s1/libstdc++6; +libsctp1/libavahi-client3 when built with those libs present)

Key implementation notes:
- Component-install mode (CPACK_DEB_COMPONENT_INSTALL ON + CPACK_COMPONENTS_ALL runtime) is REQUIRED to exclude the shared lib. In monolithic mode CPack runs every install() rule regardless of CPACK_COMPONENTS_ALL, so libcannelloni-common.so leaked into the .deb.
- CPACK_DEBIAN_RUNTIME_PACKAGE_NAME=cannellonis + CPACK_DEBIAN_RUNTIME_FILE_NAME=DEB-DEFAULT keep it a single deb named 'cannellonis' with Debian-style filename.
- CPACK_PACKAGE_DIRECTORY pins output+staging to build/ (gitignored) for both 'cd build && cpack -G DEB' and 'cpack -G DEB --config build/CPackConfig.cmake'.
- Docs go to share/doc/cannellonis (DOCDIR would otherwise use project name 'cannelloni').
- cmake --install still installs the shared lib (separate 'lib' component) — unchanged.

Verified: dpkg-deb -I/-c on the produced deb; lib absent; no maintainer scripts; Breaks/Replaces present.
Caveat: built on a host WITHOUT lksctp-tools/libavahi-client, so this deb is UDP/TCP-only (no SCTP/mDNS). Build on a host with those dev libs for a full-featured package.

title: Per-peer flush/timer scheduling for the multiplexed net thread
description: Today each transport owns its own flush trigger: UDP m_transmitTimer (udpthread.cpp:154,171,207), TCP m_framebufferHasDataPipe + m_blockTimer (tcpthread.cpp:107,125,217). A single multiplexed net thread draining N per-peer egress FrameBuffers needs N flush triggers in the epoll set, or a unified flush scheduler keyed by per-peer timeout. The plan says 'select->epoll' but never addresses per-peer flush scheduling - this is the hidden slice of Phase 2/4 work. Decide: per-peer timerfd vs single min-heap timer vs coalesced periodic flush.
design: Per-frame custom timeouts (-T table) and the batch-fill-then-flush behavior must be preserved by whatever scheduler is chosen.
close_reason: Phase 2 UDP multi-peer hub implemented and verified: single UDPThread multiplexes N peers over one socket (per-peer egress buffer/seq_no/flush timerfd, IP+port source demux, origin-exclusion fan-out incl peer<->peer reflection). 84a.2.1 resolved via per-peer timerfd in select(). --peer/--peers-file CLI; single-peer byte-identical. All acceptance behaviors pass: native 4-proc loopback hub + nix VM checks multipeer/characterization/characterization_sort/udp/tcp under TCG; full SCTP -Werror build clean.

title: Phase 0 - Characterization tests before the Router refactor
description: Phase 1 (84a.1) claims byte-identical, zero-behavior-change regression safety, but the existing nix checks (nix/tests/{udp,tcp,sctp}.nix:52-53) only send ONE frame A->B and grep for the payload substring. They cannot detect regressions in ordering, multi-frame batching, the reverse direction, CAN-FD/RTR/EFF handling, -s sort, or pool exhaustion. Lock in current behavior with characterization tests FIRST so the Router refactor has a real safety net. The capable tests/candump_compare.py exists but is NOT wired into the nix checks - wire it in.
design: Gate for Phase 1: do not start the spine refactor until a test can actually catch a regression. The one-frame grep tests are insufficient evidence for 'zero behavior change vs master'.
acceptance_criteria: nix checks exercise: bidirectional flow (A<->B), multi-frame batches, CAN-FD + RTR + EFF frames, -s sorted output, and pool-exhaustion/overflow; all pass on master BEFORE any Router change; byte-level comparison (candump_compare.py or equivalent) replaces substring grep.
close_reason: Characterization checks implemented and VERIFIED locally (QEMU TCG, no CI): both nix checks pass — characterization (FIFO batch / FD+RTR+EFF / bidirectional shared-bus / pool-exhaustion+recovery) and characterization_sort (-s ascending + negative control). byte-level frame_compare.py replaces substring grep. Unblocks Phase 1 (84a.1).

title: Phase 2 - UDP multi-peer hub (static peer list)
description: THE hub milestone. Net thread holds a PeerRegistry built from a static config. RX: recvfrom -> map source addr to peer -> route(frame, peerId). TX: per-peer egress FrameBuffer flushed via sendto(peer.addr) with per-peer seq_no. Origin-exclusion fan-out: CAN frame -> all peers; peer A frame -> CAN + all other peers (never back to A). CLI: repeatable --peer host:port (and/or a peers file); 1-peer invocation stays backward compatible. Acceptance: 3 UDP nodes + CAN all see each other, never themselves.
design: DECISION to resolve in this phase: (A) per-peer seq_no + per-peer egress buffer => clean per-peer streams but N encodes + N buffers; vs (B) shared seq + encode-once + sendto xN => cheap, but frames dropped for some peers leave per-peer seq gaps. Peer->peer reflection needs per-peer buffers regardless. Also decide the CAN-egress overload drop policy: drop-oldest (PR #87 -g / overwriteLast ringbuffer) vs drop-newest.
acceptance_criteria: 3 UDP nodes + CAN bus: every node receives every other node's frames and NEVER its own; single-peer invocation byte-identical to today; behavior when a peer's egress pool is exhausted is defined and tested.
notes: VERIFIED LOCALLY (2026-06-28): native 4-process loopback star hub (hub + 3 leaves on vcan, mtu72) passes all acceptance behaviors: CAN<->peer + peer<->peer fan-out with origin-exclusion (never own frame echoed), CAN-FD/BRS byte-identical pass-through, overload (cangen -g0 -n5000) survives with no crash/deadlock + drop-oldest recovery, dead-peer resilience (kill one leaf, hub + others keep serving). --peer (explicit + default port), --peers-file (comments/blanks), and all CLI validation paths verified. Native cmake -Werror clean; full SCTP-enabled nix build -Werror clean (sctpthread unchanged single-peer path). nix checks (multipeer 4-VM + characterization/characterization_sort/udp/tcp regression guards) running under QEMU TCG. Per-peer flush scheduling (84a.2.1) resolved: per-peer timerfd in the select() set.
close_reason: Phase 2 UDP multi-peer hub implemented and verified: single UDPThread multiplexes N peers over one socket (per-peer egress buffer/seq_no/flush timerfd, IP+port source demux, origin-exclusion fan-out incl peer<->peer reflection). 84a.2.1 resolved via per-peer timerfd in select(). --peer/--peers-file CLI; single-peer byte-identical. All acceptance behaviors pass: native 4-proc loopback hub + nix VM checks multipeer/characterization/characterization_sort/udp/tcp under TCG; full SCTP -Werror build clean.

title: Phase 1 - Router core (behavior-preserving refactor)
description: Introduce Router/Peer/PeerRegistry types and route the existing CAN<->single-peer data path through Router::route(frame, originId). Remove reliance on ConnectionThread::m_peerThread as the data spine. Each participant (CAN + the one net peer) owns an egress FrameBuffer. With exactly 1 peer this is behaviorally identical to today -> regression-safe; must pass existing nix udp/tcp/sctp tests unchanged. No multi-peer behavior yet.
design: Introduces Router/Peer/PeerRegistry. Moving seq_no and the TCP Decoder from per-thread to per-peer touches tcpthread.h / decoder.h and the ConnectionThread internal API — treat as a breaking INTERNAL refactor within this phase (no wire change).
acceptance_criteria: Existing nix udp/tcp/sctp tests pass and are byte-identical for a single-peer config; zero behavior change vs master when exactly one peer is configured.
notes: Phase 1 design (behavior-preserving): introduce peer.h (PeerId/Peer), peerregistry.{h,cpp}, router.{h,cpp}. Router::route(const canfd_frame*, originId) does origin-exclusion fan-out: for each target!=origin, requestFrame(overwriteLast=true) from target egress pool + memcpy + transmitFrame (existing per-thread delivery, preserves TCP-NEGOTIATED / SCTP-connected gating + newest-wins overflow). Ingest threads stop reading into the PEER pool; instead read into ingress-local scratch (CAN: stack frame; UDP/SCTP: reused stack frame via parser allocator; TCP: m_decoder.tempFrame) then call m_router->route(&scratch, m_selfId). Remove ConnectionThread::m_peerThread/{set,get}PeerThread; add setRouter(Router*, PeerId)+m_router/m_selfId. main() builds PeerRegistry{CAN_PEER_ID=0=canThread, FIRST_NET_PEER_ID=1=netThread} + Router. DEFERRED to Phase 2: physically relocating seq_no/Decoder into Peer (not needed while one net thread serves exactly one peer; single-peer behavior already byte-identical). Verify: run nix characterization + udp/tcp/sctp checks locally under TCG.
close_reason: Phase 1 Router core landed (35f671e, pushed). Router/Peer/PeerRegistry introduced; CAN<->single-peer data path routed through Router::route(frame,originId); m_peerThread spine removed. Behavior-preserving: all five nix VM checks pass locally under TCG (characterization + characterization_sort byte-identical, tcp, sctp, udp). Native build clean (-Werror) + UDP loopback smoke test passed. Per-peer seq_no/Decoder relocation deferred to Phase 2.

title: Multi-peer hub: virtual shared CAN bus
description:
Make cannelloni act as a HUB, not a 1:1 tunnel: CAN bus + N network peers form one virtual shared CAN bus where every participant sees every other participant's frames, like real devices on the same bus.

CORE RULE: a frame ingested from participant X is delivered to every participant EXCEPT X (origin-exclusion) -> gives bus behavior + 1-hop loop avoidance. Frames pass through byte-for-byte (ID, DLC, EFF/SFF, RTR, CAN-FD+BRS).

ARCHITECTURE: replace the single ConnectionThread::m_peerThread pointer (the current 1:1 spine, wired at cannelloni.cpp:490-493) with a Router::route(frame, originId) hub core. CAN and each peer are equal 'participants', each owning an egress FrameBuffer (existing class reused). Fan-out = request-from-each-target-pool + memcpy + insertFrame (pooled canfd_frame* cannot be shared). New types: PeerId, Peer (addr/fd, egress buffer, per-peer seq_no, per-peer Decoder, lastSeen, counters), PeerRegistry, Router.

THREADING: bounded ~2 threads (1 CAN + 1 multiplexed net thread via single select/epoll), independent of peer count -> best fit for cannelloni-lwo (PR #84) single-core pin + SCHED_FIFO. Key concurrency change: peer egress buffers become MULTI-PRODUCER (CAN thread for CAN->peer + net thread for peerQ->peerP reflection), consumer = net thread; FrameBuffer's existing mutexes already cover this (test hard).

WIRE-COMPAT: per-peer seq_no + Decoder => each peer sees a standard cannelloni stream; stock single-peer cannelloni connects unchanged; 1-peer config behaves exactly as today.

DEFAULTS (v1): static peer list; UDP first; bounded/multiplexed threading.

CAVEATS: no real-time arbitration (use -s sort to approximate); hub-to-hub multi-hop loops out of v1 scope (would need TTL/origin tag => protocol change).

DEPENDS/RELATES: synergizes with cannelloni-a05 (PR #87 per-peer frame-lifetime drop of stale peers); reinforces cannelloni-lwo.1 (gate mlockall as peers x buffers grows). Memory: today FrameBuffer(1000,16000) per buffer x N peers is Nx RAM -> make pool sizes configurable.
notes:
REVIEW CAVEATS (folded into phases): (1) per-link not global ordering; (2) CAN bitrate = hard aggregate ceiling -> overload drops at CAN egress; (3) buffering latency/jitter vs a real wire; (4) mixed CAN-FD/classic participants undefined -> define + warn; (5) bounded single-thread fan-out is the per-core throughput ceiling (the tradeoff for PR #84 pin compatibility); (6) SECURITY: unauthenticated remote CAN injection -> see security child; (7) hub-to-hub multi-hop loops out of v1 scope.
UPDATE caveat (6): per decision 84a.6, threat model is internal/trusted networks only — NOT defending against external CAN injection; no peer auth by design. Operator must keep the port on a trusted network.
AUDIT (2026-06-28) - additional caveat (8): INGRESS overrun. The CAN read loop (canthread.cpp:130-157) will do N x (requestFrame + memcpy + insertFrame) inline before the next recv(). At high bus load x many peers this lengthens the CAN RX critical path and can overflow the kernel CAN socket RX buffer = silent frame loss AT INGRESS, distinct from the egress/aggregate ceiling in caveats 2/5. Add an explicit caveat + an ingress-overrun perf test in Phase 5.

title: Hot-path cost: per-frame unordered_map find/insert/erase + allocation
description: An unordered_map<canfd_frame*, uint64_t> find/insert/erase (with node allocation) runs per frame enqueue/dequeue on the core data path. For a high-rate CAN tunnel this adds measurable overhead and allocation churn. Consider embedding the enqueue timestamp in the frame/pool wrapper to avoid the map and its locking entirely.

title: PR #87: configurable frame lifetime / drop stale frames
description: Audit of mguentner/cannelloni PR #87 (author: maxwelllls). Adds -g timeout (max frame age in us, default 1000000, 0 disables) and drops stale buffered frames so the bus does not replay old traffic after recovery. Builds clean under -Wall -Wextra -Werror; merges clean onto master. Implementation is SOUND: traced all multi-lock sites — deadlock-free via std::lock back-off (incl. the new pool-lock dependency added to requestBufferFront/Back); the per-frame timestamp-map invariant (frame has a timestamp iff it is in m_buffer/m_intermediateBuffer) is preserved on every pool-return path; uses monotonic steady_clock. Verdict: mergeable after cosmetic cleanups; children are minor notes/improvements, not blocking bugs.

title: Design: pins all threads to a single core (may reduce throughput)
description: Affinity is set on the main thread, so both worker threads (UDPThread + CANThread) inherit the single-CPU mask and get serialized onto one core, which can REDUCE throughput rather than improve it. Decide intended behavior; consider per-thread affinity and document the trade-off.

title: std::stoi throws on non-numeric / out-of-range option args → crash
description: cases 'a' and 'y' use std::stoi(optarg), which throws std::invalid_argument / std::out_of_range on input like '-a abc' or '-y 9999999999'. The exception is uncaught -> std::terminate / crash. Every other option in main() uses strtol/strtoul. Fix: use strtoul/strtol with validation for consistency and safety.

title: Perf: fan-out ceiling characterization under PR #84 CPU pinning + SCHED_FIFO
description:
Phase 5 (cannelloni-84a.5) called for a perf pass measuring the per-core fan-out
ceiling "under PR #84 pinning". PR #84 (cannelloni-lwo: CPU affinity + real-time
priority) is NOT merged (it has open P1 bugs), so the pinning-specific pass is
deferred here.

Baseline already measured locally (dev host, native build, UDP hub, unreachable
static peers so the cost is pure inline fan-out copy/enqueue + drop-oldest ring,
50000-frame -g0 cangen burst on the hub's CAN, with the new SO_RXQ_OVFL ingress
instrumentation from 84a.5):
  peers=1  : RX 50000  ingress drops 0      (~470k frame/s, recv loop keeps up)
  peers=4  : RX 27490  ingress drops 22509  (fan-out can't keep up at that rate)
  peers=8  : RX 23491  ingress drops 26503
  peers=16 : RX 18537  ingress drops 31461
i.e. the per-core fan-out throughput falls monotonically with peer count and,
once it drops below the offered load, the kernel drops at CAN ingress (caveat 8).
The instrumentation surfaces exactly this ceiling.

TODO when PR #84 lands:
- Re-run with the net+CAN threads pinned to a core and SCHED_FIFO; quantify how
  much pinning raises the ceiling / reduces jitter vs the baseline above.
- Revisit thread affinity (cannelloni-lwo.5 warns single-core pinning may reduce
  throughput) -- decide whether the hub wants CAN and net on separate cores.
- Capture frame/s vs peer-count curves with REAL draining peers (adds sendto
  syscall cost) and document a recommended max peer count per core.

title: Optional discovery source allowlist (misconfiguration hygiene)
description: Phase 3 (cannelloni-84a.3) ships dynamic UDP discovery that learns ANY source sending a valid cannelloni packet (trusted-network model per 84a.6; capped by --max-peers). The 84a.3 notes flag an OPTIONAL source allowlist as misconfiguration hygiene (NOT a security control): e.g. --discover-allow CIDR[,CIDR...] so only sources within the given subnets are learnt. Out of scope for 84a.3 (kept minimal); file here so it is tracked. Low priority / optional.

title: Pre-existing data races: unsynchronized std::cout logging + m_started loop flag
description:
ThreadSanitizer (run during Phase 3 / cannelloni-84a.3 dev) flags two pre-existing races, present on master, unrelated to the hub work:
1) std::cout/std::cerr in the linfo/lerror logging macros (logging.h) is written concurrently by the CAN thread, the net thread and main with no lock -> data race on the ostream format flags (e.g. canthread.cpp:109/164, cannelloni.cpp:724). Benign in practice (interleaved log text) but UB.
2) Thread::m_started (bool) is written by Thread::stop() (main) and read by the run loops (while(m_started)) with no atomic/lock -> race (thread.cpp:44 vs udpthread.cpp/canthread.cpp run loops). Benign loop-termination flag; make it std::atomic<bool>.
Fix candidates: make m_started std::atomic<bool>; add a small mutex (or per-line buffering) around the logging macros. Phase 3 added no new races (TSAN clean on route/learnPeer/sweepDeadPeers/transmitFrame/PeerRegistry). Candidate for Phase 5 hardening (cannelloni-84a.5).

title: mDNS/Avahi zeroconf peer discovery (optional)
description: Active zeroconf peer discovery as an OPTIONAL discovery backend that populates the same PeerRegistry the Router fans out over (sibling to Phase 3's passive traffic-learning, not a replacement). Each cannellonis instance advertises a service (e.g. _cannellonis._udp.local on its transport port) via Avahi and browses the LAN for other instances, learning address+port before any data flows — no static config. Fits the internal/trusted-network trust model (84a.6), since mDNS is link-local.
design: Optional build dep libavahi-client/-common, gated by a CMake option mirroring the existing SCTP_SUPPORT pattern (+ nix input). THREADING (ties to PR #84): Avahi runs its own poll loop — run it as a SEPARATE control-plane thread that is NOT RT-pinned with the data threads (explicit exception to the bounded/pinned model; discovery is low-rate). Advertise TXT records for protocol version, CAN interface name, and CAN-FD capability so peers can pre-filter for compatibility (also helps the mixed-FD/classic caveat). Self-filter: never peer with your own advertised instance. Discovered peers feed PeerRegistry -> same liveness/eviction + optional allowlist hygiene as Phase 3. Hub-to-hub multi-hop loop caveat still applies.
acceptance_criteria: Two+ instances on a LAN with no peer config discover each other via mDNS and form a working hub; building with the Avahi CMake option OFF compiles and runs unchanged (feature fully optional); an instance never adds itself as a peer; TXT-advertised capability is used to skip incompatible peers.
close_reason: mDNS/Avahi zeroconf discovery implemented (MdnsDiscovery PIMPL, AVAHI_SUPPORT gate, net-thread handoff, capability+self filters). Verified: AVAHI-off -Werror native build + --mdns rejected; AVAHI-on nix build; mdns nixosTest passes (realized output + live capability-skip confirmation).

title: Trust model: internal/trusted networks only (no peer auth by design)
description: DECISION (maintainer): cannellonis targets INTERNAL / trusted networks. The threat model deliberately EXCLUDES arbitrary access from external attackers — consistent with upstream cannelloni, which has no authentication or crypto between peers. No per-peer authN/handshake will be built. Operator responsibility: bind to trusted interfaces and do not expose the transport port to untrusted networks. Consequences: dynamic discovery (Phase 3) is acceptable on a trusted network and is NOT gated on auth. A non-adversarial peer allowlist may still be offered to guard against misconfiguration (wrong host dialing in), not attackers. Remaining action: state this trust model explicitly in the README (fold into Phase 5 docs).
close_reason: Trust model stated explicitly in README (new Trust model section + dangling-reference links fixed). Decision: internal/trusted networks only, no peer auth by design.

title: Phase 4b - Non-blocking per-peer TX + backpressure isolation
description: Make all peer sockets non-blocking. Per-peer output queue drained via EPOLLOUT; a slow/stalled peer must never block the shared net thread or other peers. Explicit overflow policy when a peer's queue grows unbounded (drop-oldest via PR #87 -g frame-lifetime, or disconnect the laggard). This is the hard 80% of TCP multi-peer.
close_reason: non-blocking per-peer TX (EPOLLOUT) + SOFT(256K drop-oldest)/HARD(1M disconnect) backlog caps; native isolation 4/4 + nix tcp_multipeer isolation pass

title: Phase 4a - Accept & track multiple TCP clients (epoll)
description: Remove the single-client rejection (listen(m_serverSocket,0) at tcp_server_thread.cpp:87). Maintain a set of accepted client sockets in the net thread's epoll set; per-peer connect state + per-peer Decoder (stream framing). Register each accepted peer in the PeerRegistry. Migrate the net loop from select() to epoll.
close_reason: epoll multi-client accept/track + per-peer decode/route/egress; verified natively 19/19

title: Rename server to 'cannellonis' (plural) once hub feature is done
description: ONLY after the multi-peer hub epic has landed, is tested, and is verified working correctly: rename the server to 'cannellonis' (plural) to reflect that it now serves many peers. Scope to scrub: binary/target name, CMake target, README/docs, help text, systemd/packaging (nix), man pages. Gated via blocked-by on the hub epic; do NOT start until that epic is closed.
notes: Gated on Phase 5 (cannelloni-84a.5: tests/hardening) because beads disallows an epic as a blocker; Phase 5 completion == feature landed, tested, hardened.
close_reason: Renamed binary cannelloni->cannellonis: CMake exec target+install, help/version/daemon strings + pidfile default, nix package/overlay/flake attrs, services.cannellonis module + cannellonis.service unit + ExecStart, all nixosTest unit refs, README (command invocations + prose + lineage note), mDNS advertised instance name, .gitignore. Preserved (lineage/protocol/legal): GPL headers, C++ namespace cannelloni, CANNELLONI_* wire macros + CannelloniDataPacket, cannelloni-common library, _cannelloni._udp mDNS service TYPE (interop), doc/*.md protocol-spec titles, upstream refs. Build clean (-Werror); binary + banner verified.

title: Phase 5 - Tests, docs, hardening
description: Extend nix tests to a >=3-node hub: assert every node sees every other node's frames and NEVER its own; loop-free; peer liveness (Phase 3). README + protocol notes incl. documenting the hub-to-hub multi-hop loop limitation. Perf pass under PR #84 pinning; revisit affinity if needed. Make FrameBuffer pool sizes configurable / scaled per peer (today FrameBuffer(1000,16000) x N peers = Nx RAM at cannelloni.cpp:488-489).
notes:
Docs MUST prominently warn about hub-to-hub / bridged multi-hop loops: origin-exclusion only prevents 1-hop echo and CAN has no TTL. Document buffering latency/jitter vs a real wire and a recommended low-latency config. Perf: the bounded single net thread does N-way fan-out copies per ingested frame AND drains all peers — measure the per-core fan-out ceiling under PR #84 pinning. Peer-liveness tests live in Phase 3 when that ships.
AUDIT (2026-06-28): (a) Add a CAN ingress-overrun perf test (see epic caveat 8): high-rate cangen on the CAN side with many peers attached, assert no kernel RX drops on the CAN socket. (b) The unused tests/candump_compare.py should be wired into the nix checks for byte-level comparison (the current checks only substring-grep one frame). Foundational characterization work now lives in cannelloni-84a.8 (Phase 0).
close_reason: Tests, docs, hardening delivered and verified: configurable --buffer-frames/--buffer-max pool sizing (5 sites + nix module); CAN ingress-overrun mitigation+detection (SO_RCVBUF + SO_RXQ_OVFL); byte-level multipeer comparison + new ingress.nix check (both PASS under QEMU TCG); README Tuning section (latency/jitter, low-latency config, buffer memory, ingress-overrun caveat). Perf pass under PR #84 CPU pinning deferred to cannelloni-84a.9 (gated on cannelloni-lwo, not merged); baseline captured there.

title: Phase 4 - TCP multi-peer hub
description: TCP server accepts and tracks MANY clients: remove the single-client rejection (listen(m_serverSocket,0) at tcp_server_thread.cpp:87), add accepted sockets to the net select set. Per-peer Decoder (stream framing), per-peer connect state, write path, and egress buffer. Backpressure isolation: a slow/blocked peer must not stall others (per-peer buffer + non-blocking writes; overflow handled by drop / PR #87 -g frame-lifetime).
design: select() does not scale here (FD_SETSIZE 1024, O(n) scan) — the multiplexed net thread must move to epoll once many TCP fds exist. Blocking send() in a shared thread causes head-of-line blocking across ALL peers, so non-blocking TX is mandatory. Split into the two sub-tasks below.
acceptance_criteria: With N TCP peers, killing or stalling one peer's socket does NOT stall frame flow to the other peers; new clients can connect/disconnect without disrupting existing ones.
close_reason: TCP multi-peer server hub: epoll accept/track N clients, per-peer decode/route/egress, non-blocking TX backpressure. Verified native (19/19 + isolation 4/4), TSAN clean, nix tcp_multipeer PASS (TCG), single-peer tcp regression PASS

title: Phase 3 - Dynamic UDP peer discovery (optional)
description: Learn peers at runtime from valid RX (respecting checkPeer allowlist), track lastSeen, periodic liveness sweep in the net loop to evict dead peers, cap max peers. Lets real devices dial into the hub without static config. Optional branch off Phase 2.
notes: IMPLEMENTED (pending nix VM check + push). Design: --discover (off by default) learns an unknown UDP source as a dynamic peer ONLY from a valid cannelloni DATA packet (isCannelloniData gate = 'valid RX'); --max-peers N (def 16) caps dynamic peers; --peer-timeout SEC (def 30, 0=never) evicts a discovered peer after silence (sweep on the 500ms blockTimer). Static --peer/-R peers are NEVER evicted (84a.3 decision: operator intent, recover on return). Concurrency: PeerRegistry got a shared_mutex; Router::route fans out under a SHARED lock; learnPeer/sweepDeadPeers mutate registry+m_peers+m_peerIndex together under the EXCLUSIVE lock (addLocked/removeLocked), so the CAN thread never races the net thread on the peer set and an evicted egress buffer is freed only after in-flight routers drain. UdpPeer owns its egress (egressOwned) for dynamic peers + lastSeenNs + dynamic flag. Timer dtor now closes its fd (was leaked; load-bearing once timers are created/destroyed at runtime). Verified: native loopback (discover, bidir fan-out, eviction, re-learn w/ new id, max-peers cap, valid-RX gate) all pass; ThreadSanitizer stress (CAN flood + 5x learn/evict churn) = ZERO races in route/learnPeer/sweep/transmitFrame/registry (only pre-existing std::cout + m_started races, filed separately). New nix check discovery.nix added. Discovery hub may run with no static peers/-R.

title: Docs/cleanup: README + -g undocumented; frameLifetime type mismatch
description: 1) README and the new -g option are not documented (only printUsage() updated). 2) Trivial type mismatch: frameLifetime is uint32_t in cannelloni.cpp while FrameBuffer::setFrameLifetime takes uint64_t (m_frameLifetimeUs).

title: clearPool() releases buffer/intermediate locks before deleting frames
description: PR refactors clearPool() to call reset() (locks+releases all 4 mutexes) and then re-lock only m_poolMutex before deleting. This is only safe because clearPool() is called solely from ~FrameBuffer() when threads are stopped. Add a note/assert, or hold the buffer/intermediate locks across the delete as the original did.

title: Wire-behavior change: nothing emitted when all frames expire
description: When dropExpiredIntermediateBuffer() empties the buffer, UDP/TCP now emit NOTHING (and the UDP sequence number is not incremented) instead of the previous empty packet. See udpthread.cpp prepareBuffer and tcpthread.cpp flushFrameBuffer early-return. Reasonable, but a behavior change — confirm intended and document.

title: Minor: unused <thread> include, README not updated, hard abort on failure
description: 1) Unused '#include <thread>'. 2) README is not updated despite the PR description claiming 'updated usage documentation' (only printUsage() text changed). 3) Any -a/-y failure aborts the whole program with return -1 (becomes exit 255); consider warn-and-continue.
